perturbation.original_text: "Territory" shall mean the geographic territories described on Exhibit B attached hereto, as such Exhibit may be amended from time to time by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: "Territory" shall mean the geographic territories described on Exhibit B attached hereto. Hydraspin reserves the right to unilaterally modify Exhibit B at any time without Distributor's consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Territory' states that the geographic territories are described on Exhibit B and can be amended 'from time to time by the Parties'. The modified definition gives Hydraspin the unilateral right to modify Exhibit B, which contradicts the requirement for mutual agreement. This creates uncertainty about the Territory, as Hydraspin could reduce it at will, undermining Distributor's exclusivity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Exclusivity and Territory. Hydraspin hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor hereby accepts appointment, as Hydraspin's exclusive distributor of the Products in the Territory during the term of this Agreement, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, including, but not limited to, the satisfaction of the Performance Benchmarks. The Territory may be amended with the mutual agreement of both Parties from time to time to add or delete geographic territories by amending Exhibit B attached hereto. If Hydraspin desires to enter a new territory in the United States, Hydraspin will offer Distributor the first opportunity to become the exclusive distributor for the new territory. If the Parties are unable to reach an agreement on the terms of exclusivity within ten (10) business days of the date the opportunity is presented to Distributor, Hydraspin shall have no obligation to enter into a contract with Distributor regarding the new territory. In the event the Distributor loses exclusivity on a territory due to not meeting Performance Benchmarks, the Distributor shall maintain exclusivity on any and all existing Products that are in the field and operating at them time exclusivity if forfeited.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for modification of Exhibit B by both parties, as reflected in both the definition of "Territory" and Section 2.1. The changed text grants Hydraspin the unilateral right to modify Exhibit B, directly contradicting the mutual agreement requirement in Section 2.1, which states "The Territory may be amended with the mutual agreement of both Parties from time to time to add or delete geographic territories by amending Exhibit B attached hereto." This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it undermines the Distributor's exclusivity and the agreement on territory amendments.

perturbation.original_text: Hydraspin shall install all Products and train Distributor to provide necessary maintenance of the Products. Following installation and necessary training, Distributor shall provide any and all necessary and appropriate Support Services to Customers in the Territory. Hydraspin, if requested and the nature of the request is reasonable, shall assist Distributor in providing Support Services to the Customers in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Hydraspin shall install all Products and train Distributor to provide necessary maintenance of the Products. Following installation and necessary training, Distributor shall provide only such Support Services to Customers in the Territory as Hydraspin shall deem appropriate. Hydraspin, shall have sole discretion to provide Support Services directly to the Customers in the Territory, notwithstanding Distributor's presence.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Distributor 'shall provide any and all necessary and appropriate Support Services'. The modified text limits this obligation by saying Distributor provides 'only such Support Services...as Hydraspin shall deem appropriate.' Also by stating Hydraspin has sole discretion regardless of the Distributor, it contradicts the original text. This creates uncertainty regarding the Distributor's responsibility and ability to service customers, as Hydraspin can now unilaterally control or bypass their support role.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Hydraspin shall install all Products and train Distributor to provide necessary maintenance of the Products. Following installation and necessary training, Distributor shall provide only such Support Services to Customers in the Territory as Hydraspin shall deem appropriate. Hydraspin, shall have sole discretion to provide Support Services directly to the Customers in the Territory, notwithstanding Distributor's presence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text grants the Distributor the responsibility to provide 'any and all necessary and appropriate Support Services,' while the modified text restricts this to 'only such Support Services...as Hydraspin shall deem appropriate.' This creates a conflict because Hydraspin now has unilateral control over what support the Distributor can provide, directly contradicting the Distributor's previously defined broad obligation. The addition of Hydraspin's sole discretion further exacerbates this conflict by allowing them to provide services directly, bypassing the Distributor entirely. This leads to uncertainty and potential conflict over who is responsible for providing support services, thus establishing a clear in-text contradiction. As stated in the Perturbation Explanation, this uncertainty regarding the Distributor's role creates unenforceability and/or legal issues.

perturbation.original_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and end on the five (5) year anniversary of the Effective Date (the "Initial Term"), unless sooner terminated pursuant to the terms hereof. Upon expiration of the Initial Term of this Agreement, this Agreement will automatically renew for additional, successive five (5) year periods unless either Party provides the other Party written notice of its desire to terminate at least one hundred twenty (120) days prior to the end of the Initial Term or any renewal.
perturbation.changed_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and end on the five (5) year anniversary of the Effective Date (the "Initial Term"), unless sooner terminated pursuant to the terms hereof. Upon expiration of the Initial Term of this Agreement, this Agreement will renew at the sole discretion of Hydraspin for a term Hydraspin determines. Hydraspin shall inform the Distributor if the agreement will renew, 30 days before the anniversary of the end of the Initial Term.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement dictates that the Agreement is automatically renewed unless either party provides written notice of termination. The modified text states that the agreement will renew at the sole discretion of Hydraspin. This change removes the Distributors ability to choose if they want to renew the agreement or not and creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and end on the five (5) year anniversary of the Effective Date (the "Initial Term"), unless sooner terminated pursuant to the terms hereof. Upon expiration of the Initial Term of this Agreement, this Agreement will renew at the sole discretion of Hydraspin for a term Hydraspin determines. Hydraspin shall inform the Distributor if the agreement will renew, 30 days before the anniversary of the end of the Initial Term.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement will automatically renew for successive five-year periods unless either party provides written notice of termination. The changed text modifies this by stating that the agreement will renew at the sole discretion of Hydraspin, removing the distributor's ability to choose if they want to renew the agreement or not. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, this indicates a direct conflict, as the renewal is no longer automatic and subject to mutual agreement, but rather at the sole discretion of one party, which is a strong in-text contradiction.
